Name: curvature_aware_quantization
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Mixed-Precision Quantization
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that incorporating curvature-aware precision routing into mixed-precision quantization can lead to significant improvements in model accuracy and computational efficiency. By analyzing the curvature of the loss landscape, we can identify optimal precision levels for different layers and operations, reducing the need for exhaustive search and improving the overall performance of the model.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT have explored training-free proxies, adaptive mixed-precision frameworks, and post-training quantization methods for mixed-precision quantization. However, these approaches often rely on heuristics or manual configurations, and may not fully capture the underlying geometry of the loss landscape. Our approach builds upon these works by incorporating curvature-aware precision routing, which provides a more principled and efficient way to optimize precision levels.
Abstract: Mixed-precision quantization has emerged as a promising technique for improving the computational efficiency of deep neural networks. However, existing approaches often rely on exhaustive search or heuristics to determine optimal precision levels, which can be time-consuming and suboptimal. In this work, we propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework that incorporates the geometry of the loss landscape to identify optimal precision levels for different layers and operations. Our approach is based on a rigorous mathematical formulation, which provides a principled way to optimize precision levels and reduce the need for exhaustive search. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through extensive experiments on various benchmarks, showing significant improvements in model accuracy and computational efficiency.
Experiments: We will conduct experiments on various benchmarks, including ImageNet and CIFAR-10, using different neural network architectures, such as ResNet and MobileNet. We will evaluate the performance of our curvature-aware precision routing framework against state-of-the-art baselines, including EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT. We will use metrics such as accuracy, FLOPs, and energy efficiency to evaluate the performance of our approach. We will also conduct ablation studies to analyze the impact of different components of our framework on the overall performance.
Risk Factors and Limitations: One potential risk factor is the computational cost of computing the curvature of the loss landscape, which can be expensive for large models. To mitigate this risk, we will explore efficient approximation methods and parallelization techniques to reduce the computational cost. Another potential limitation is the need for careful hyperparameter tuning, which can be time-consuming and require significant expertise. To address this limitation, we will develop a systematic hyperparameter tuning protocol and provide detailed guidelines for practitioners to follow.
